'''Connor Wrench''' (born 4 October 2001) is an English [[rugby league]] [[footballer]] who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].

==Playing career==
===Warrington Wolves===
Wrench made his Super League debut in round 14 of the [[Super League XXV results|2020 Super League season]] for Warrington against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/salford-vs-warrington/teams/54568|title=Starting Lineups - Salford vs Warrington|publisher=Sky Sports |date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1587/round-14-fixtures-confirmed|title=Round 14 fixtures confirmed|publisher=Sky Sports |date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/54329439|title=Super League: Salford Red Devils 20-18 Warrington Wolves |publisher=BBC Sport |date=29 September 2020}}</ref>

Wrench played eight games for Warrington in the 2023 Super League season as Warrington finished sixth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs elimination playoff loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23827325.highs-lows-warrington-wolves-2023-season/|title=Highs and lows from Warrington Wolves' 2023 season|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk}}</ref>

On 26 August 2025 his immediate retirement was announced <ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=26 Aug 2025|title= Warrington centre Wrench retires at 23|url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c3v30ewngr0o}}</ref>

===Newcastle Thunder (loan)===
On 6 May 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=6 May 2021|accessdate=7 May 2021|title=Warrington pair head to Newcastle on loan|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-pair-head-to-newcastle-on-loan/}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 6 March 2026 it was reported that he had come out of retirement and signed for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=6 Mar 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Connor Wrench secures Super League return after retirement U-turn|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/connor-wrench-super-league-return}}</ref>
